sentence: Subjective:
The patient, a 30-year-old female, presents with a chronic history of asthma symptoms, including persistent coughing and wheezing, which she attributes to her asthma.
She reports frequent exacerbations, typically triggered by respiratory infections, and has required multiple urgent care visits and one emergency department visit in the past year.
She has been prescribed albuterol inhaler (Ventolin) for rescue use, which provides temporary relief, but she has not been consistent with her treatment regimen.
She denies smoking, vaping, or marijuana use and has no history of seasonal allergies.
She has a past medical history of one hospitalization for asthma at age 8, but no further hospitalizations since then.
Review of systems:
The patient reports no issues with seasonal allergies, but notes that her asthma symptoms worsen when she is exposed to cats.
Current medications:
Albuterol inhaler (Ventolin) 2 puffs as needed for rescue use.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 128/82, respiratory rate 16, oxygen saturation 99% on room air.
Physical exam: Diminished lung sounds with occasional slight expiratory wheeze bilaterally.
Neck is supple, trachea is midline, no lymphadenopathy, carotid bruit, or clubbing.
Test Results: Pulmonary function test results are consistent with asthma.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has moderate intermittent asthma, requiring consistent use of an inhaled steroid inhaler to prevent daily symptoms and reduce reliance on her rescue inhaler.
Problem list: Asthma; moderate intermittent; confirmed.
Plan:
I prescribed Flovent 110 micrograms, one puff twice daily for the prevention of asthma symptoms.
She can continue to use her albuterol inhaler (Ventolin) 2 puffs every 4-6 hours as needed for rescue use.
I will create an asthma action plan to help manage her symptoms and prevent future exacerbations.
I will see her in a month to review her progress and adjust her treatment plan as needed.
Follow-up:
I will see the patient in a month to review her symptoms and adjust her treatment plan as necessary.
